headline: Mozart Drama Hits Fresh Notes: Sharpe and Bettany Reimagine Classical Rivalry
lede: Bravo's bold new limited series transforms the legendary Mozart-Salieri narrative into a razor-sharp character study of musical obsession and creative genius.
body:
Forget everything known about the tired Mozart-Salieri trope. Bravo's provocative new limited series "Maestro's Duel" electrifies the classical music landscape with Will Sharpe and Paul Bettany delivering career-defining performances.

Director Michael Gracey orchestrates a stunning deconstruction of historical mythology, stripping away romantic legends to reveal two complex artists wrestling with professional jealousy and artistic ambition. Sharpe portrays Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart as a mercurial, brilliant upstart whose raw talent threatens musical establishment norms, while Bettany's Antonio Salieri emerges as a technically precise composer consumed by creative inadequacy.

The series distinguishes itself through meticulous period details and psychological depth rarely attempted in classical music narratives. Each episode peels back layers of professional competition, revealing how artistic rivalry can simultaneously destroy and inspire creative minds.

Production design captures 18th-century Vienna with breathtaking authenticity, transforming musical backstage drama into something resembling a high-stakes psychological thriller. Costume and set designers collaborated extensively to create an immersive world that feels simultaneously historical and immediate.
key_facts: Bravo's first classical music limited series
Starring Will Sharpe and Paul Bettany
